instruction: Create a detailed incident response playbook for the following cyber incident.
input:
Incident type: Ransomware
Target asset: Windows AD Server
Detection source: EDR Alert - Suspicious File Encryption
Initial vector: Email Attachment (Phishing)
Tactics and techniques:
- Initial Access: Phishing
- Execution: User Execution
- Impact: Data Encrypted for Impact
Severity: High
Tags: malware, lateral_movement, windows, ransomware, phishing

Organization assets:
- ws-corp-pool: Dell OptiPlex 7090 workstation, corporate (10.10.1.0/24), managed by CrowdStrike Falcon, 150 Windows 11 workstations, medium criticality
- srv-dc-01: Dell PowerEdge R750 server, datacenter (10.20.1.10), primary domain controller, managed by CrowdStrike Falcon, Primary AD DS, critical criticality
- srv-dc-02: Dell PowerEdge R750 server, datacenter (10.20.1.11), secondary domain controller, managed by CrowdStrike Falcon, Secondary AD DS, critical criticality
- edr-console: CrowdStrike Falcon edr, cloud (cloud), endpoint detection and response, Covers all endpoints and servers, critical criticality
- siem-splunk-01: Splunk Enterprise 9.2 siem, datacenter (10.20.2.10), centralized log aggregation and alerting, 90-day hot retention - 1-year warm, critical criticality
- fw-perimeter-01: Palo Alto PA-850 firewall, dmz (10.0.0.1), perimeter filtering, Managed via Panorama, critical criticality
- fw-internal-01: Palo Alto PA-450 firewall, datacenter (10.20.0.1), east-west segmentation, Segments corporate from datacenter, high criticality
- backup-veeam-01: Veeam Backup and Replication 12 backup, datacenter (10.20.4.10), primary backup server, Daily full backups - 4-hour RPO, critical criticality
output:
Playbook:

Phase: Identification
Action: Triage alert, confirm IOC via edr-console, snapshot affected host on ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24)
Tools: CrowdStrike Falcon, Velociraptor
Target response time (minutes): 15

Phase: Containment
Action: Isolate host from network via fw-perimeter-01 (10.0.0.1) and fw-internal-01 (10.20.0.1), disable compromised user account on srv-dc-01 (10.20.1.10) and srv-dc-02 (10.20.1.11), monitor logs on siem-splunk-01 (10.20.2.10)
Tools: edr-console, siem-splunk-01, fw-perimeter-01, fw-internal-01
Target response time (minutes): 10

Phase: Eradication
Action: Remove malicious binaries, patch vulnerabilities on ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24) and servers srv-dc-01 (10.20.1.10) and srv-dc-02 (10.20.1.11)
Tools: YARA, Patching Platform
Target response time (minutes): 60

Phase: Recovery
Action: Restore from backups on backup-veeam-01 (10.20.4.10), monitor for reinfection on siem-splunk-01 (10.20.2.10)
Tools: Veeam, siem-splunk-01
Target response time (minutes): 120

Phase: Lessons Learned
Action: Conduct IR debrief, update detection rules on edr-console and siem-splunk-01, user awareness training
Tools: Google Docs, MISP
Target response time (minutes): 240

Affected Assets:
- ws-corp-pool: Affected host
- edr-console: EDR management
- siem-splunk-01: Log aggregation and alerting
- fw-perimeter-01: Perimeter filtering
- fw-internal-01: East-west segmentation
- srv-dc-01: Primary domain controller
- srv-dc-02: Secondary domain controller
- backup-veeam-01: Backup server

Final status: Resolved
Total response duration (minutes): 445

Organization assets:
- srv-web-01: Nginx on Ubuntu 22.04 web_server, dmz (10.0.0.20), public-facing web application, Behind Palo Alto WAF policy, high criticality
- srv-db-01: Microsoft SQL Server 2022 database, datacenter (10.20.3.10), primary business database, Contains customer PII - encrypted at rest, critical criticality
- fw-perimeter-01: Palo Alto PA-850 firewall, dmz (10.0.0.1), perimeter filtering, Managed via Panorama, critical criticality
- ids-core-01: Suricata 7.0 ids, datacenter (10.20.0.5), intrusion detection, Feeds alerts to Splunk, high criticality

Playbook:

Phase: Identification
Action: Analyze siem-splunk-01 logs, identify compromised endpoints using edr-console

Affected Assets:
- srv-web-01: Web server

- fw-perimeter-01: Perimeter filtering

- edr-console: Endpoint detection and response

Tools: hping3, siem-splunk-01
Target response time (minutes): 150

Affected Assets:

Incident type: Supply Chain Attack
Target asset: Third-Party Software
Detection source: SIEM Alert - Anomalous Process Execution
Initial vector: Compromised Software Update
Tactics and techniques:
- Initial Access: Supply Chain Compromise
- Execution: Trusted Relationship
Severity: Critical
Tags: supply_chain, malware, third_party

Organization assets:
- edr-console: CrowdStrike Falcon edr, cloud (cloud), endpoint detection and response, Covers all endpoints and servers, critical criticality
- siem-splunk-01: Splunk Enterprise 9.2 siem, datacenter (10.20.2.10), centralized log aggregation and alerting, 90-day hot retention - 1-year warm, critical criticality
- pam-vault-01: CyberArk PAS pam, datacenter (10.20.1.50), privileged access management, All admin credentials vaulted here, critical criticality
- mfa-provider: Duo Security mfa, cloud (cloud), multi-factor authentication, Enforced for VPN - email - and admin consoles, critical criticality
- backup-offsite: AWS S3 Glacier dr_site, cloud (cloud), offsite backup copy, 30-day retention - immutable, critical criticality
- srv-db-01: Microsoft SQL Server 2022 database, datacenter (10.20.3.10), primary business database, Contains customer PII - encrypted at rest, critical criticality
- ndr-sensor-01: Darktrace ndr, datacenter (10.20.0.8), network traffic analysis, Monitors east-west and north-south traffic, high criticality
output:
Playbook:

Phase: Identification
Action: Analyze siem-splunk-01 logs, validate compromised software on srv-db-01 and other endpoints connected to 10.20.3.10
Tools: siem-splunk-01, VirusTotal
Target response time (minutes): 20

Phase: Containment
Action: Isolate affected systems like srv-db-01 with IP 10.20.3.10, block vendor domains using ndr-sensor-01 with IP 10.20.0.8
Tools: edr-console, ndr-sensor-01
Target response time (minutes): 15

Phase: Eradication
Action: Remove compromised software from srv-db-01, verify integrity of pam-vault-01 with IP 10.20.1.50
Tools: YARA, Tenable
Target response time (minutes): 60

Phase: Recovery
Action: Revert to trusted versions on srv-db-01 with IP 10.20.3.10, monitor for anomalies using siem-splunk-01 and edr-console
Tools: SCCM, siem-splunk-01
Target response time (minutes): 120

Phase: Lessons Learned
Action: Audit supply chain, update vendor policies for mfa-provider and backup-offsite
Tools: Google Docs, MISP
Target response time (minutes): 180

Affected Assets:
- siem-splunk-01: Centralized log aggregation and alerting
- srv-db-01: Primary business database
- edr-console: Endpoint detection and response
- ndr-sensor-01: Network traffic analysis
- pam-vault-01: Privileged access management
- mfa-provider: Multi-factor authentication
- backup-offsite: Offsite backup copy

Final status: Resolved
Total response duration (minutes): 395

instruction: Create a detailed incident response playbook for the following cyber incident.
input:
Incident type: Malware Infection
Target asset: Windows Endpoint
Detection source: EDR Alert - Suspicious Process
Initial vector: Drive-by Download
Tactics and techniques:
- Initial Access: Drive-by Compromise
- Execution: Malicious File Execution
Severity: High
Tags: malware, windows, drive_by

Organization assets:
- ws-corp-pool: Dell OptiPlex 7090 workstation, corporate (10.10.1.0/24), managed by CrowdStrike Falcon, 150 Windows 11 workstations, medium criticality
- srv-dc-01: Dell PowerEdge R750 server, datacenter (10.20.1.10), primary domain controller, managed by CrowdStrike Falcon, Primary AD DS, critical criticality
- srv-dc-02: Dell PowerEdge R750 server, datacenter (10.20.1.11), secondary domain controller, managed by CrowdStrike Falcon, Secondary AD DS, critical criticality
- edr-console: CrowdStrike Falcon edr, cloud (cloud), endpoint detection and response, Covers all endpoints and servers, critical criticality
- siem-splunk-01: Splunk Enterprise 9.2 siem, datacenter (10.20.2.10), centralized log aggregation and alerting, 90-day hot retention - 1-year warm, critical criticality
- fw-perimeter-01: Palo Alto PA-850 firewall, dmz (10.0.0.1), perimeter filtering, Managed via Panorama, critical criticality
- fw-internal-01: Palo Alto PA-450 firewall, datacenter (10.20.0.1), east-west segmentation, Segments corporate from datacenter, high criticality
- backup-veeam-01: Veeam Backup and Replication 12 backup, datacenter (10.20.4.10), primary backup server, Daily full backups - 4-hour RPO, critical criticality
output:
Playbook:

Phase: Identification
Action: Confirm malware on ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24), collect IOCs from edr-console and siem-splunk-01 (10.20.2.10)
Tools: CrowdStrike Falcon, VirusTotal
Target response time (minutes): 15

Phase: Containment
Action: Isolate endpoint ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24) using edr-console, block C2 communications through fw-perimeter-01 (10.0.0.1) and fw-internal-01 (10.20.0.1)
Tools: CrowdStrike Falcon, Palo Alto PA-850, Palo Alto PA-450
Target response time (minutes): 10

Phase: Eradication
Action: Remove malware from ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24), update AV on srv-dc-01 (10.20.1.10) and srv-dc-02 (10.20.1.11)
Tools: YARA, Microsoft Defender
Target response time (minutes): 45

Phase: Recovery
Action: Reimage endpoint ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24), monitor network using siem-splunk-01 (10.20.2.10) and restore from backup-veeam-01 (10.20.4.10) if necessary
Tools: SCCM, Splunk Enterprise 9.2
Target response time (minutes): 90

Phase: Lessons Learned
Action: Update web filters on fw-perimeter-01 (10.0.0.1), train users on ws-corp-pool (10.10.1.0/24)
Tools: Google Docs, MISP
Target response time (minutes): 120

Affected Assets:
- ws-corp-pool: Endpoint for malware analysis and remediation
- edr-console: EDR system for threat detection and response
- siem-splunk-01: SIEM system for log aggregation and alerting
- fw-perimeter-01: Perimeter firewall for blocking C2 communications
- fw-internal-01: Internal firewall for east-west segmentation
- srv-dc-01: Primary domain controller for AV updates
- srv-dc-02: Secondary domain controller for AV updates
- backup-veeam-01: Backup server for data restoration

Final status: Resolved
Total response duration (minutes): 280

instruction: Create a detailed incident response playbook for the following cyber incident.
input:

Target asset: Windows Server

- Command and Control: Encrypted Channel
Severity: High

Organization assets:
- fw-perimeter-01: Palo Alto PA-850 firewall, dmz (10.0.0.1), perimeter filtering, Managed via Panorama, critical criticality
- fw-internal-01: Palo Alto PA-450 firewall, datacenter (10.20.0.1), east-west segmentation, Segments corporate from datacenter, high criticality
- ids-core-01: Suricata 7.0 ids, datacenter (10.20.0.5), intrusion detection, Feeds alerts to Splunk, high criticality
- ndr-sensor-01: Darktrace ndr, datacenter (10.20.0.8), network traffic analysis, Monitors east-west and north-south traffic, high criticality
- router-wan-01: Cisco ISR 4451 router, dmz (10.0.0.2), WAN gateway, Dual ISP failover, high criticality
- switch-core-01: Cisco Catalyst 9300 switch, datacenter (10.20.0.2), core switching, Trunk ports to all datacenter VLANs, high criticality
- siem-splunk-01: Splunk Enterprise 9.2 siem, datacenter (10.20.2.10), centralized log aggregation and alerting, 90-day hot retention - 1-year warm, critical criticality

output:
Playbook:

Phase: Identification
Action: Analyze traffic from ndr-sensor-01 (10.20.0.8) and ids-core-01 (10.20.0.5), confirm C2 communication through router-wan-01 (10.0.0.2) and fw-perimeter-01 (10.0.0.1)

Phase: Recovery

Target response time (minutes): 90

Action: Restrict PowerShell, update policies, and review web filtering rules on proxy-web-01
Tools: Google Docs, MISP
Target response time (minutes): 120

Affected Assets:

- proxy-web-01: Web filtering and SSL inspection policy updates

instruction: Create a detailed incident response playbook for the following cyber incident.
input:
Incident type: Ransomware
Target asset: Internet-facing Windows servers and enterprise email infrastructure
Detection source: MITRE ATT&CK Campaign - HomeLand Justice
Initial vector: Exploit Public-Facing Application
Tactics and techniques:
- Initial Access: Exploit Public-Facing Application (T1190)
- Execution: PowerShell (T1059.001)
- Persistence: Web Shell (T1505.003)
- Credential Access: LSASS Memory (T1003.001)
- Impact: Data Encrypted for Impact (T1486)
Severity: Critical
Tags: ransomware, mitre_attack
output:
Playbook:
Phase: Identification
Action: Monitor system logs for suspicious activity, analyze network traffic for unusual patterns, and inspect internet-facing Windows servers for signs of exploitation. Investigate alerts from security information and event management (SIEM) systems, such as Splunk, for potential indicators of compromise (IOCs) related to the HomeLand Justice campaign. Utilize endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools like CrowdStrike Falcon to identify and isolate affected systems.
Tools: Splunk, CrowdStrike Falcon, Palo Alto
Target response time (minutes): 15

Phase: Containment
Action: Isolate affected internet-facing Windows servers and email infrastructure from the rest of the network to prevent lateral movement. Block all incoming and outgoing traffic to and from the affected systems using firewalls like Palo Alto. Implement temporary access controls, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA), to prevent further unauthorized access. Utilize tools like Carbon Black to monitor endpoint activity and prevent potential data exfiltration.
Tools: Palo Alto, Carbon Black, CrowdStrike Falcon
Target response time (minutes): 30

Phase: Eradication
Action: Remove the web shell and any other malware from the affected systems. Utilize anti-virus software like Symantec Endpoint Protection to scan for and remove malware. Restore affected systems from backups or rebuild them from scratch, ensuring all software is up-to-date and patched. Use tools like PowerShell to remove any suspicious scripts or code.
Tools: Symantec Endpoint Protection, PowerShell, CrowdStrike Falcon
Target response time (minutes): 60

Phase: Recovery
Action: Restore encrypted data from backups, if available. Utilize data recovery tools like Veritas NetBackup to restore data from backups. Rebuild or replace affected systems, ensuring all software is up-to-date and patched. Implement additional security controls, such as regular security audits and penetration testing, to prevent similar incidents in the future.
Tools: Veritas NetBackup, CrowdStrike Falcon, Splunk
Target response time (minutes): 120

Phase: Lessons Learned
Action: Conduct a thorough post-incident analysis to identify root causes and areas for improvement. Utilize tools like Tableau to analyze incident response data and identify trends. Develop and implement additional security controls, such as regular security awareness training, to prevent similar incidents in the future. Document lessons learned and update incident response playbooks accordingly.
Tools: Tableau, Splunk, CrowdStrike Falcon
Target response time (minutes): 240

Final status: Resolved
Total response duration (minutes): 465
